pattern: Every original value appears in exactly two of the defining equations, so XOR-ing every equation together cancels the unknowns and leaves a single checkable condition.
hints: Write down derived[i] = original[i] xor original[(i+1) mod n] for every i, and imagine XOR-ing all n equations together.
Each original[i] shows up in exactly two of those equations (once as the left term, once as the right term of its neighbor), so it cancels out of the combined XOR entirely.
That leaves XOR of the whole derived array on one side and 0 on the other -- a valid original exists exactly when that total XOR is 0, with no need to build original at all.

edge_inputs.description: three elements with a valid reconstruction

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    derived = [rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)]
    return [derived]
